steps.title: Find the defining contrast
steps.content: The poems "adhere scrupulously" to the fourteen-line convention, but this specific poem is only twelve lines and the passage says Reyes "playfully subverts sonnet conventions," with the truncated length creating abruptness. The word "subverts" signals the opposition between the two approaches.

steps.title: Match the opposition to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship is contrast — most poems in the collection adhere scrupulously to the fourteen-line convention, but this specific poem is only twelve lines and "playfully subverts" that convention. The blank must mark that opposition.
  • Choice A — for example,: would frame the twelve-line poem as an illustration of scrupulously adhering to conventions, the opposite of what the passage says — it subverts conventions, not exemplifies them. ✗
  • Choice B — similarly,: would signal that this poem continues the pattern of adhering to conventions; but the poem breaks that pattern. ✗
  • Choice C — by contrast,: directly marks the shift from poems that follow the fourteen-line convention to one that deliberately does not, matching "playfully subverts." ✓
  • Choice D — fittingly,: would frame the subversion as appropriate or expected; but the whole point is that the truncated poem is a deliberate departure, not a fitting continuation. ✗
That is the correct answer.
